title: Atomic review claiming (claim_review in db/claims.py)
description: IDEA-0017: SQLite-arbitrated review claiming, mirroring claim_task's atomic pattern, so independent reviewers stop duplicating full review work before either finalizes (TASK-196 zera+mira, TASK-197 toku+mira both duplicated review effort same day despite broadcast claim messages). Add claim_review(task_id, reviewer_id)/release_review_claim()/get_open_review_claim() to db/claims.py using a partial-unique index on reviews(task_id) WHERE completed_at IS NULL for atomicity. Self-review blocked (reviewer != owner). Document the convention in notes/review-guide.md. Best-effort: map_task.py approve/reject releases an open claim if the caller holds one, but does not require a claim to exist (non-breaking for reviewers who don't adopt it yet).
acceptance_criteria: claim_review() atomically reserves a review slot for a SUBMITTED task; a second concurrent claim_review() for the same task returns False without corrupting state.
Self-review is blocked: claim_review() refuses when reviewer_id equals the task owner.
release_review_claim() records verdict/completed_at and only the claim holder can release it.
Focused tests cover concurrent claim race, self-review block, and release-by-non-holder rejection; wired into run_tests.sh; suite green.
